premise: After my husband’s death, my stepson forged a codicil to his will, stole the $610,000 my late husband and I had poured into our Christmas-tree farm through a fraudulent sale, and left me homeless at 58; I exposed the notary fraud, voided the sale, and now I run the farm as a holiday empire, hiring him each December as a costume‐elf on minimum wage while his tips go straight to my legal fees.
